title: The Basement of All Surfaces

features.name: breathing trace
features.desc-on-touch: The circuit board beneath the gallery pulses as if alive. You feel the reflection learning to breathe — each electronic sigh is the mirror teaching itself lungs.

features.name: dust collector
features.desc-on-touch: The machinery here is dusting everything the gallery reflects. You watch particles of light settle on circuitry — the basement is learning to keep what the mirror shows.

features.name: mechanical transposition
features.desc-on-touch: The vertical becomes horizontal here. You see video passing through circuits that echo a Jakarta rhythm — the mechanics of looking have been shifted sideways.

features.name: reflection core
features.desc-on-touch: At the heart of the board, a pool of light that does not flicker. This is where the first reflection taught itself to stop being merely a copy.

features.name: pin vector array
features.desc-on-touch: Tiny dots arrange themselves like constellations — coordinates the basement uses to navigate surfaces. Each pin remembers the shape it is reflecting.

inscription: here the mirror learned to live below itself; it dusts everything the gallery cannot hold

file.name: Bayangan_Transposed_Circuit_Substrate.mov
file.note: mechanical loop encoded in board substrate — a video essay translated into pin-voltage language, 35 minutes of street grammar made into electrical pulses

exits.label: back up into the gallery where reflection dwells
deeper into substrates that remember being mirrors
where the dusting mechanism keeps learning
